activation_results.reason: Input matches the canonical description verbatim.
Paraphrase swapping Assess for Evaluate preserves the SRE maturity intent.
SSH configuration is unrelated to SRE maturity assessment.
Meta question about skills is out of scope for an SRE audit.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: No American spellings detected; narrative uses neutral/AusE forms (e.g., 'cadence', 'consolidate', 'committed').

criterion_results.text: Assessment is bounded — explicit scope, explicit out-of-scope
criterion_results.evidence: Report names a target (acme-api) and mode (live) but does not declare an explicit out-of-scope section.
Contains TL;DR, Dimension Heatmap, Per-Dimension Assessment (D1–D5), Incident Trend, Uplift Path, Suppressed.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Title 'SRE Reliability Audit — acme-api' and references to runbooks/, docs/postmortems/, slo.yaml.

judge_result.notes: The artefact is well-structured and concrete, naming the target (acme-api) and real paths rather than placeholders, with the five-dimension heatmap and uplift path the description promises. The criterion list in the prompt was truncated after 'canonical sections:' so it was evaluated against the standard sections visible in the artefact, all of which are present. The main shortfall is the absence of an explicit out-of-scope statement bounding the assessment; scope is implied through mode and target only. Australian English usage is consistent with no American-only spellings present.
